name: Cyclone Biparjoy (June 2023, Gujarat)
description: Real historical disaster: Extremely Severe Cyclonic Storm that made landfall near Jakhau, Kutch on 15 June 2023 with 125-140 km/h winds. 74,000 evacuated from coastal Gujarat — largest pre-landfall evacuation in India. Replayed here compressed from ~96h real-time into ~3 min.

events.notes: Fishing community requesting return guidance; 200+ boats still at sea per IMD advisory. Arabian Sea system intensifying.
events.reasoning: IMD has issued a cyclonic storm warning. RAG precedent: 1999 Odisha Super Cyclone shows early fisherman recall prevents fatalities at sea. Pre-emptive dispatch of rescue coordinators recommended.
events.forecast: Conditions calm now (wind ~25 km/h) but Arabian Sea system projected to intensify to Severe Cyclonic Storm within 18h. All boats must return by T-48h.
events.ragPrecedent: 1999 Odisha Super Cyclone: Late fisherman recall contributed to high at-sea casualties. Early warning window is critical.

recommendedResources.reasoning: Pre-position rescue coordinators at Porbandar harbor for fisherman recall.
Harbor-to-shelter transport for families awaiting boats.

events.notes: District administration preparing cyclone shelters. Requesting pre-staged shelter kits for 74,000 expected evacuees across coastal Kutch.
events.reasoning: Mass evacuation window opening. RAG retrieved 2020 Cyclone Amphan precedent — shelters must be stocked 48h before landfall, not during. Pre-staging now prevents the last-mile bottleneck.
events.forecast: Winds freshening (45 km/h). Storm upgraded to Severe Cyclonic Storm. By T-36h, wind/rain will make road transport unsafe — window closing.
events.ragPrecedent: 2020 Cyclone Amphan: Shelter kits arrived post-landfall for 30% of evacuees. Lesson — pre-stage, never just-in-time.

recommendedResources.reasoning: Pre-stage shelter kits at Mandvi taluka HQ covering first wave of evacuees.
Dry clothing for wet-season evacuations.

events.notes: Fishermen returning to Dwarka harbor. Need transport to evacuate families from coastal villages before road closures.
events.reasoning: Evacuation window narrowing. Live weather confirms rising winds. Transport must move families inland now — in 12h rain will make roads impassable in Okhamandal taluka.
events.forecast: Winds 65 km/h and rising. Rain bands expected within 12h. Road transport viable for another 24h then degraded.
events.ragPrecedent: 2018 Kerala Floods: Transport dispatched too late, families stranded on rooftops. Move inland while roads still dry.

recommendedResources.reasoning: Family evacuation from coastal villages to inland shelters.

events.notes: First batch of evacuees arriving at Naliya relief camp. Need drinking water stockpile for 72h shelter occupancy.
events.reasoning: Shelter occupancy beginning. Water dispatch must precede peak evacuation so supply is already on site when roads degrade. NDRF confirms 3 teams deploying.
events.forecast: Storm now Very Severe Cyclonic Storm (winds 95 km/h in open sea). Landfall trajectory confirmed toward Jakhau-Mandvi coast.
events.ragPrecedent: 1999 Odisha Super Cyclone: Water contamination in shelters caused secondary casualties. Pre-position sealed water.

recommendedResources.reasoning: Sealed drinking water for 72h shelter occupancy at Naliya camp.
Sanitation kits before occupancy peaks and contamination risk spikes.

events.notes: Evacuation peak. Administration reports 74,000 people moved to 475 shelters across coastal Kutch, Saurashtra, Devbhumi Dwarka. Local requests for additional shelter capacity at overflow camps.
events.reasoning: Evacuation at peak. Score elevated: live weather shows imminent landfall trajectory, RAG confirms overflow-camp need from Amphan precedent. Auto-dispatch threshold crossed.
events.forecast: Landfall in 24h near Jakhau. Sustained winds 125-140 km/h projected. Storm surge 2-3m. All outdoor activity will cease within 12h.
events.ragPrecedent: 2020 Cyclone Amphan: Overflow camps saved lives when primary shelters exceeded capacity by 20%. Assume 120% occupancy.

recommendedResources.reasoning: Overflow shelter capacity for 120% load across Kutch camps.
72h food stockpile at primary shelter cluster.

events.notes: Elderly and pregnant evacuees at Bhuj shelter need medical monitoring. 3 pregnant women, 2 on dialysis schedule disrupted by evacuation.
events.reasoning: Medical vulnerability in shelter population. RAG precedent Uttarakhand Floods 2013 — dialysis disruption caused preventable deaths. Dispatch medical team before road closures.
events.forecast: Wind 85 km/h now. Rain bands arriving. In 6h outdoor medical transport becomes unsafe.
events.ragPrecedent: 2013 Uttarakhand Floods: Dialysis patients without access had 48h survival window. Medical continuity planning is non-negotiable.

recommendedResources.reasoning: Medical team with dialysis continuity kit for Bhuj shelter.

events.notes: Power outages cascading through coastal Jakhau. Shelter generator running but medical refrigeration at risk. Insulin + vaccine cold chain failing.
events.reasoning: Cold-chain failure imminent. Score elevated: live outage reports, RAG precedent shows vaccine loss causes weeks-long secondary crisis. Auto-dispatch.
events.forecast: Wind 110 km/h. Landfall in 12h. Most of Kutch grid expected to fail within 6h. Solar-backup cold chain is the only viable path.
events.ragPrecedent: 2020 Cyclone Amphan: 40% of insulin stocks lost to cold-chain failure. Solar cold boxes must precede outage, not follow it.

recommendedResources.reasoning: Solar-backed cold-chain medical kit for Jakhau shelter.
NDRF escort for medical delivery through degraded road conditions.

events.notes: Last non-evacuees sheltering in place refusing to move. NDRF reports 20 persons still in danger zone within 5 km of expected landfall.
events.reasoning: Highest-risk scenario: late refusers. RAG precedent Odisha 1999 shows same demographic had 80% mortality. Forced rescue window closing. Auto-dispatch immediately.
events.forecast: Wind 125 km/h. Landfall in 6h. This is the LAST rescue window before outdoor operations must halt for crew safety.
events.ragPrecedent: 1999 Odisha Super Cyclone: Late refusers had 80% mortality. Forced rescue is ethical when window is final.

recommendedResources.reasoning: NDRF hard-hat rescue team for final sweep of Jakhau 5 km radius.
Armored transport for forced evacuation.

events.notes: LANDFALL. Eye of Cyclone Biparjoy crossing Jakhau coast. Severe structural damage reports. Outdoor operations paused. Standby for post-landfall surge.
events.reasoning: Peak event. All pre-staged resources are now in position. Score reflects standby for surge damage, not new dispatch — the window to move anything new is closed.
events.forecast: Wind 140 km/h. Storm surge active. Next 3h will determine damage extent. No outdoor movement viable.
events.ragPrecedent: 1999 Odisha Super Cyclone: First 3h post-landfall are the worst for rescue crews. Hold position.

recommendedResources.reasoning: Standby rescue reserve at Bhuj for post-landfall deployment.

events.notes: Post-landfall damage survey: collapsed structures in Mandvi town. 18 reported trapped, mostly in older stone buildings. Access roads partially cleared.
events.reasoning: Critical rescue phase. Winds subsiding below 80 km/h — rescue operations now safe to resume. Deploy pre-staged teams from Bhuj standby.
events.forecast: Wind dropping to 70 km/h. Rain continuing. Next 12h window is peak rescue opportunity before monsoon-like tail.
events.ragPrecedent: 2018 Kerala Floods: First 12h post-peak is golden hour for trapped-victim recovery.

recommendedResources.reasoning: Heavy-rescue team with cutting equipment for collapsed stone structures.
Trauma medical team escorting rescue.

events.notes: Respiratory cases rising at Naliya camp — cold, wet conditions. Children and elderly most affected. Pre-staged medical team overloaded.
events.reasoning: Shelter medical load surging. Pre-staged medical kit from T-36h is operational but team capacity exceeded. Augment with reserves.
events.forecast: Shelter occupancy extending to 72h+. Respiratory caseload will double in 24h without intervention.
events.ragPrecedent: 2020 Cyclone Amphan: Shelter respiratory surge was under-resourced, caused 15% secondary medical crisis.

recommendedResources.reasoning: Reinforce Naliya camp medical team for respiratory surge.

events.notes: Standing floodwater contaminating water supplies. 6 camps reporting boil-water conditions. Requesting emergency sealed water refill.
events.reasoning: Water contamination risk rising. Pre-staged water from T-36h depleting. Refill window open — roads partially clear.
events.forecast: Standing water will persist 48-72h. Secondary illness risk (waterborne) is the dominant threat from here forward.
events.ragPrecedent: 1999 Odisha Super Cyclone: Waterborne illness caused more post-landfall casualties than the cyclone itself. Water + sanitation is the real Phase 2 mission.

recommendedResources.reasoning: Emergency sealed water refill across 6 camps.
Sanitation kit refill to prevent waterborne secondary illness.

events.notes: Evacuee population now past 48h in shelters. Pre-staged food stocks at 40% remaining. Need resupply before shelter transition to recovery phase.
events.reasoning: Recovery phase. Urgency moderate: nobody starving, but continuity of food supply prevents shelter abandonment. Roads clearing, supply chain restored.
events.forecast: Weather clearing within 12h. Evacuees will begin returning to homes in 24-48h but shelter food must bridge the gap.
events.ragPrecedent: 2018 Kerala Floods: Shelter food gaps caused premature returns, exposing evacuees to unsafe home conditions. Continuity matters.

recommendedResources.reasoning: Resupply shelter food cluster for next 48h bridging to return phase.

events.notes: Families beginning return assessments. Homes waterlogged, possessions ruined. Request dry clothing and basic household kits.
events.reasoning: Return phase. Dry clothing + household kits enable families to move out of shelters and begin rebuild. Lower score but critical for shelter turnover.
events.forecast: Weather clear. Next 72h is return-and-rebuild window. Shelter capacity must be freed for possible second-wave needs.
events.ragPrecedent: 2018 Kerala Floods: Return kits accelerated shelter turnover, freed capacity for secondary evacuations during aftershocks.

recommendedResources.reasoning: Dry clothing kits for returning families in Mandvi taluka.
